description: Wraps a reader (like File) and adds buffering, which reduces the number of system calls. Essential for reading files line-by-line efficiently. Without BufReader, each read call goes directly to the OS. Python equivalent: io.BufferedReader (Python's open() uses buffering by default).

methods.name: new
methods.description: Creates a new BufReader with a default buffer size (8 KB) wrapping the given reader.

methods.name: lines
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the lines of the reader. Each line is a Result<String> with the newline stripped. Python equivalent: for line in file.

methods.name: read_line
methods.signature: fn read_line(&mut self, buf: &mut String) -> Result<usize>
methods.description: Reads a single line including the newline character into the provided buffer. Returns the number of bytes read. From the BufRead trait.

methods.name: read_until
methods.signature: fn read_until(&mut self, byte: u8, buf: &mut Vec<u8>) -> Result<usize>
methods.description: Reads bytes until the specified delimiter byte is found, appending them to buf. Useful for reading until a custom separator.

methods.name: with_capacity
methods.signature: fn with_capacity(capacity: usize, inner: R) -> BufReader<R>
methods.description: Creates a new BufReader with the specified buffer capacity. Use this when the default 8 KB buffer is not optimal for your use case.
